observations.recommendation: We recommended to the LCE to demand the creditor/s to settle the account the soonest possible time to avoid inconveniences, pursuant to Section 4 (4) of P.D. No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

We recommended the management to furnished the Audit Team Contract documents for infrastructure projects within five (5) working days from the date the contract was perfected, in compliance to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, in order for the audit team to consider necessary review and evaluation of such and informing the management of some defects/deficiencies, if any.

We recommended that Management take full opportunity on the
application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in
COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of physical
count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, and
disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, if any, for the purpose
of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile their records pertaining to the subject unserviceable
PPEs particularly the cost and carrying amounts of these PPE
items, which will be subsequently dropped from the books of
accounts after its disposal;
b. Direct the Property Unit to prepare the IIRUP for all
unserviceable property, and the WMR for all waste materials,
subject for disposal;
c. Create a committee responsible for the appraisal of unserviceable
property and supplies, whose appraisal shall be considered as the
floor price in the case of disposal thru sale at public auction; and
d. Direct the General Services Officer to dispose of the unserviceable
property and supplies in accordance with the rules and regulations
on supply and property management in local government units.

We recommended that the Management to faithfully adhere to the
rules and regulations provided under Section 20 of the MNGAS for
LGUs relative to the establishment/set-up of real property tax
receivable at the beginning of the year to have a fair presentation of
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the management to submit necessary documents in
connection with infrastructure projects (5) working days from the
date the contract was perfected in compliance with the provision of
Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that LCE instruct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Faithfully observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting
for inventories as provided under the MNGAS.
b. Secure to have a stockroom provided for the inventory items of the
agency to protect them from spillage/spoilage and unauthorized
use.
c. Stop the practice of recording/charging regular purchases of
supplies and materials immediately to the expense accounts and
should maintain on a current basis in the General Ledger and
Inventory Account for all purchases of supplies and materials for
stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the
accounting period. Issuance thereof shall be recorded based on the
Report of Supplies and Materials Issued.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reclassify the PPE accounts below ₱50,000.00 threshold to its
appropriate semi-expendable properties and expenses; and
b. Adjust the opening balance in the Statement of Changes in Net
Assets/Equity in compliance to the COA Circular No. 2022-004,
dated May 31, 2022.

We recommended that the General Services Officer facilitate the
issuance of the accompanying ICS of the semi-expendable properties,
pursuant to Section 4.1 of the COA Circular No. 2022-004, dated May
31, 2022.

In view of the foregoing, we
recommended that the LCE,
to faithfully:
a) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each year.
b) Require the General Services Officer shall maintain and update her property cards per category of PPE, pursuant to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update his property ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
d) Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal Accountant reconcile their property cards and property ledger cards, respectively.
We recommended to the accountable officers to adhere comply to COA Circular 2012-001, Section 4(6) of PD 1445, and of Section 7.1 of R.A. 9184, for the legality/regularity of the transactions.

We recommended to require the General Services Officer to post bond equal to the estimated amount of property under his possession, control and custody, respectively, in compliance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019.

The management shall strictly comply with the Section 100 PD 1445: Reports of disbursing officers in a government agency and the Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code: Failure of accountable officers to render accounts – in submission of the regular report promptly in time frame given.

We recommended the management to furnished the Audit Team Contract documents for infrastructure projects within five (5) working days from the date the contract was perfected, in compliance to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, in order for the audit team to consider necessary review and evaluation of such and informing the management of some defects/deficiencies, if any.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to demand the
creditor/s to settle the account the soonest possible time to avoid
inconveniences, pursuant to Section 4 (4) of Presidential Decree
No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), strictly:
a. Inventory Committee shall prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, in compliance with
Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units
(LGUs), Volume I;
b. General Services Officer shall maintain and update his
property cards per category of property, plant and equipment,
pursuant to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. Municipal Accountant shall maintain and update his property
ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment,
pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
d. Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal
Accountant shall reconcile their property cards and property
ledger cards, respectively; and
e. Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual
detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and
submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January
31, of each year.

We therefore recommend to the accountable officers to adhere
comply to Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2012-001, Section
4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445, and of Section 7.1 of Republic
Act (R.A.) 9184, for the legality/regularity of the transactions.

We instructed the Municipal Accountant that;
a) At the lower portion of the Report of Disbursement (RD) is
summary of the cash transactions for the month which
contains the beginning balance, total cash received by the
agency, total disbursements and the ending cash in bank
balance.
b) He should prepare and submit bank reconciliation
statement.

We recommended the management to submit necessary documents in
connection with infrastructure projects (5) working days from the date the
contract was perfected in compliance with the provision of Section 3.1.1 of
COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend to require the General Services Officer to post bond
equal to the estimated amount of property under his possession,
control and custody, respectively, in compliance with Treasury Circular
No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019.

We recommended that the Property, Plant and Equipment totaling to P 366,187,538.43 ON ALL FUNDS must be evaluated, thus, submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in violation to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 105 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines is deemed necessary.

through an Automated Teller Machine (ATM) for payment of salaries to its permanent

Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the

assigned personnel to comply the above-mentioned provisions of the COA’s Manual on

1. Financial statements and other required accounting reports are made to aid management in decision making. They reveal neglected areas of operations in which remedial measures succeeding vouchers on-time. The Chief Executive committed to submit COA audit reports.

observations.recommendation: 3.We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

observations.recommendation: 4.We recommend that the Accounting Office should submit all the unaccounted original vouchers and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within seven (7) days from the receipt thereof. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Treasurer shall forward the vouchers and supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant as soon as the corresponding checks are released to claimants, in accordance with procedures laid out in the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) Manual.
observations.managementAction: 2018 Management committed to submit original supporting documents as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management should require the Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory count of Property, Plant and Equipment annually taking into consideration the required standards and procedures and invite the presence of the Commission on Audit as a witness during the conduct of inventory-taking. Further, we recommend the management to submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) CY2017 to this Commission.

We recommend that the management should set up Petty Cash Fund at the beginning of the year. The management should prepare ALOBs (Obligation Request) for the fund, recorded in the Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) and obligated as Other Expenses. A Disbursement Voucher (DV) must be prepared for replenishments of petty cash fund and supported by Petty Cash Vouchers (PCVs) and its supporting documents.

It is recommended that all payments for said expenses shall be supported with proper documentation required by the Commission for post audit. Further, other concerned officials are also advised not to approve and disburse funds of the government without first satisfying the requirement set by the law.
It was recommended that the agency should treat their claim for traveling expenses as Cash Advance and not thru reimbursement to ensure that the appropriate procedure shall be strictly adhered to.

We recommend that the payment of procured items should be made directly to the supplier/dealer and stop the practice of paying procured supplies and materials thru reimbursement and the appropriate procurement procedure shall be strictly adhered to. Furthermore, all the required documents shall be complied and complete to ensure that the approval of these disbursement vouchers should first be sought before payments are made.

We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
We recommended to call the attention of all concerned employees with unsettled Notice of Suspensions/Disallowances to settle the same within the reglementary period of time.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel concerned to settle the Notice of Suspension pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The management settled these suspensions by submitting the lacking documents required by the Commission and the Auditor already issued the Notice of Settlement for the said Notice of Suspensions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accountant and Treasurer to locate and submit the missing vouchers. The Accountant should see to it that monthly reports and journals should be submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers/payrolls/official receipts in accordance with the provisions of Section 100 of P.D. 1445. The Treasurer should also be instructed to regularly and promptly submit the Report of checks Issued (RCI) to the Office of the Auditor for proper monitoring of all check issuances. A logbook for vouchers and checks received and released should be maintained by both offices.
observations.managementAction: The management already submitted the Disbursement Vouchers together with the attaching documents of these unaccounted checks to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should also submit the Report of Disbursement, at the lower portion of the Report of Disbursement (RD) is a summary of the cash transactions for the month. It contains the beginning balance, total cash received by the agency, total disbursements and the ending cash in bank balance. We recommended that the Bookkeeper/Accounting Clerk be instructed to prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement to properly monitor the Cash in Bank Balance.
observations.managementAction: The management already prepared and submitted the Bank Reconciliation Statements for calendar year 2016 to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all pertinent DVs and other supporting documents related to Trust and Special Education Funds shall be submitted for audit examination. Furthermore, the same documents shall be submitted on time in the ensuing periods to faithfully comply with related provisions.
observations.managementAction: The management was able to forward to the Office of the Auditor the unsubmitted Disbursement Vouchers for transactions in Trust Fund and Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned LGU officials shall submit to this Commission all pertinent reports related to its Environmental Solid Waste Management Program to ascertain its compliance with applicable provisions of Law.
observations.managementAction: The management already prepared and submitted to this Office the Accomplishment Report on Ecological Solid Waste Management Fund of the LGU for calendar year 2016.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

The Municipal Mayor shall cause and personally supervise the prompt submission of final reports/statements and other required reports within the time frame prescribed under the existing policies.

Financial statements and other required accounting reports are made to aid management in decision making. They reveal neglected areas of operations in which remedial measures could be made. Prompt submission on reports permit the early detection of errors and deficiencies that will enable the concerned authorities to institute remedial measures. It also discourages the commission of irregularities because of early detection. The Chief Executive should personally supervise the early submission and or preparation of financial statements/reports and shall see to it that reports are submitted within the time framed.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory count of Property, Plant and Equipment annually taking into consideration the required standards and procedures. Further, we recommend the management to submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) CY2018 to this Commission.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Accounting Office should submit all the unaccounted original vouchers and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within seven (7) days from the receipt thereof. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Treasurer shall forward the vouchers and supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant as soon as the corresponding checks are released to claimants, in accordance with procedures laid out in the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) Manual.
The Municipal Mayor shall cause and personally supervise the prompt submission of final reports/statements and other required reports within the time frame prescribed under the existing policies.
observations.managementAction: The Management explained that the main cause of withholding the submission of some disbursement vouchers for a number of months is the delay on liquidation of cash advances by the concerned personnel. They committed to submit succeeding vouchers on-time.
observations.reason: Section 100 of PD 1445 which states, “Disbursing Officer in any government agency shall render monthly reports of their transactions pursuant to regulations of the Commission to be submitted not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month to the auditor concerned who shall conduct the necessary examination and audit within thirty days from receipt thereof.”. Also, section 70 and 73, vol. 1 of the New Government Accounting System, and 70 of COA Circular 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002 provides that monthly pre-closing trial balance for each fund shall be submitted not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month. Section 44 of COA Circular 92-382 also provides, among others, that the report of checks issued together with the corresponding vouchers shall be submitted to the Auditor weekly. Section 48 of the same Circular also states that the liquidation of regular cash advances should be made within five (5) days after the fifteenth/end of every month which should be submitted to the Accountant. Within ten (10) days from receipt, the Accountant shall verify the liquidation report and submit this report with the vouchers to the Auditor. For the year 2018, it was observed that the Municipal Government of Pagalungan is very much delayed in the submission of disbursement vouchers (DV) and Report of disbursements (ROD) for the 80% portion of the Internal Revenue Allotment together with the supporting documents. Aside from the fact that the submission takes place after unreasonable length of time, it was also observed that when the DVs were submitted, it has become the practice of the agency to submit them in volume, that is, the DVs submitted covers 2 or 10-month period instead of submitting them on a monthly basis. This practice resulted to the delay in the post-auditing, verification and the preparation of reports. Furthermore, the delay in the submission of DVs and delayed preparation of the Report of Disbursements (RD) by various accountable officers cause the delay in the preparation of the financial statements . Journal entries cannot be prepared in the absence of the DVs and RDs from various accountable officials. As a result, the Trial Balance cannot be prepared as well as the interim financial statements to include the Balance Sheet, Statement of Income and Expense and Statement of Cash Flows. It was gathered from the voucher in charge that they have to withhold submission of some disbursement vouchers for a number of months pending submission of lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory count of Property, Plant and Equipment annually taking into consideration the required standards and procedures. Further, we recommend the management to submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) CY2018 to this Commission.
observations.managementAction: Although the Management had not submitted Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) CY 2018 to this Commission, they explained that they actually had conducted physical count of PPEs last December 2018.
observations.reason: Property, Plant and Equipment balance shown in the Financial Statement remained to be unreliable due to the absence of supporting PPE records. The total amount still does not represent the true value of the PPE account because the General Services Office (GSO) failed to conduct physical inventory, documentation on the existence of the Property, Plant and Equipment and maintenance of complete property records. Sec. 105 of P.D. 1445 states that . . . “Every officer accountable for government property shall be liable for its money value in case of improper or unauthorized use or misapplication thereof, by himself or any person for those acts he may be responsible. He shall likewise be liable for all losses, damage and/or determination occasioned by negligence in the keeping or use of the property, whether or not it be at the time in his actual custody.” Paragraph 2 of Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs provides that physical count of property, plant and equipment by type should be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). This shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year. Review of Property, Plant and Equipment account showed that PPE were not supported with subsidiary ledger list or Inventory Report, which cause the inability of the management to validate and conduct inventory count of property.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Management responded that they will submit the required contracts and other documents related to their upcoming programs/projects.
observations.reason: We have audited the various projects of the Municipality of Pagalungan, Maguindanao and we have observed that submissions by management of purchase orders and contracts for review along with related documents are partial and incomplete, which in effect precludes proper review of procurement transactions, contrary to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 contains the following provisions: “2.1 This circular shall cover all contracts, purchase orders and the like, entered into by any government agency irrespective of amount involved. 3.1 Contracts 3.1.1 Within five (5) working days from the execution of a contract by the government or any of its subdivisions, agencies or instrumentalities, including government-owned and controlled corporations and their subsidiaries, a copy of said contract and each of all the documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation shall be furnished to the Auditor of the agency concerned. In case of agencies audited on an engagement basis, submission of a copy of the contract and its supporting documents shall be to the Auditor of the mother agency or parent company, as the case may be. 3.1.3 For technical review purposes, submission of contracts and their supporting documents shall furthermore be guided by the specific documentary requirements outlined in the attached checklist marked as Annexes “A” to “T” of this circular. 3.2 Purchase Orders 3.2.1 A copy of any purchase order irrespective of amount, and each and every supporting document, shall, within five (5) working days from issuance thereof, be submitted to the Auditor concerned. Within the same period, the Auditor shall review and point out to management defects and/or deficiencies, if any….” Section 3.1.2 thereof provides “that the copies of documents required to be submitted shall include but not limited to eligibility and bidding documents, contract agreement, performance security, notice of award, notice to proceed, program of work and detailed estimates, obligation request, BAC resolutions and minutes of proceedings, among others,” as well as “such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement and considered necessary in the auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.” The purpose of the above cited provisions is to enable the Auditor to ascertain the regularity and legality of the transactions supported by contracts at the early stage of the review/evaluation/inspection and to communicate to the management any deficiencies that maybe noted so that corrective and remedial measures can be undertaken. The failure of the management to submit the required documentary requirements to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period affect the timely audit of transactions of the Municipality and the purpose of issuances of such Circulars will be defeated as well.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Office should submit all the unaccounted original vouchers and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within seven (7) days from the receipt thereof. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Treasurer shall forward the vouchers and supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant as soon as the corresponding checks are released to claimants, in accordance with procedures laid out in the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) Manual.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to submit supporting documents as required.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Accountant failed to effect prompt submission to the Office of the Auditor of all vouchers issued by the municipality together with their supporting documents, contrary to the provisions of Section 107 of PD 1445 and the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts. Section 107 of P. D. 1445 provides that “… all accountable officers shall render their accounts, submit their vouchers, and make deposits of money collected or held by them at such times and in such manner as shall be prescribed in the regulations of the Commission.” Section 7.2.1 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) as prescribed for use under COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 placed the following responsibility, among others, on the Agency Accountant: The Chief Accountant, bookkeeper and/or other authorized official performing accounting and/or bookkeeping functions of the audited agency shall ensure that: a. The reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers are immediately recorded in the books of accounts and submitted to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month; … Our review of vouchers submitted by the agency covering the period from January 1, 2018 to December 31, 2018 disclosed that the Office of the Municipal Accountant has submitted vouchers without their proper supporting documents hereby, may be subjected to suspension or disallowance, as the case may be, of these transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality should adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording supplies. Moreover, the Accounting and Supply Offices should revisit the said Manual which details the procedures in monitoring, controlling, and recording of acquisition and disposal of inventory.

The Management should make an adjustment with regards to the appropriateness of using these accounts. The Deferred RPT has to be closed to the equity account to recognize the revenue previously unrecognized if the Management ascertains that the said amount herein referred to has sufficiently met the criteria on revenue recognition.
observations.managementAction: AA Adjusting R Entries / 2015 Reversal of Accounts
